methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
GRU is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Skyler Chan. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 13 peers: 5 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private firms including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Standard triangulation methods proved inapplicable to this valuation. Peer-median analysis was not executable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables—while 23 same-sector peers exist broadly, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than the 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring also could not be applied; though the Space & Aerospace public sector median trades at 59.8x sales across the 6 identified tickers, GRU's revenue is not disclosed—a common limitation for private-stage companies. Without revenue visibility, applying this multiple would require unsubstantiated assumptions.

The lack of applicable quantitative methods reflects the fundamental challenge of valuing early-stage frontier-tech companies: sparse comparable data at the seed stage and limited financial disclosure. A credible valuation would require either disclosure of GRU's revenue or ARR, proprietary revenue projections with explicit assumptions, or prognostic frameworks specific to seed-stage aerospace ventures. Until such data is available, standard valuation approaches cannot be reliably triangulated.
